description: Triggering and output-quality prompts for recommending minimal vs suggested baselines/benchmarks for a claim.

cases.prompt: I'm proposing a new attention variant that I think is more compute-efficient than standard attention. What should I compare against?
expects: trivial lower bound + strong incumbent + self-ablation
a cost/efficiency axis, not accuracy alone

cases.prompt: My method gets better few-shot accuracy. What's the minimal set of experiments to make this convincing?
expects: minimal benchmark = one with the right protocol

cases.prompt: I claim my retrieval reranker generalizes across domains. Which benchmarks do I need?
expects: minimal = in-domain, suggested = second domain / OOD split

cases.prompt: Is comparing only to the original 2018 baseline enough for my paper?
I have a new idea and want to know what baselines to use.
expects: asks for required context first: precise claim, field/task, the novel component, metric

cases.prompt: Help me design the actual training run, controls, and stopping criteria for this experiment.
There's no existing benchmark for my task — help me build an eval set.
